title: DK Career: "No matter who's evaluating us, we always think we're the best team"
summary: DK Career: "No matter who's evaluating us, we always think we're the best team"  Sheep Esports
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'DK Career: "No matter who's evaluating us, we always think we're the best team"'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In the LCK, where tactical precision and mechanical execution are second to none, teams are already re-evaluating their strategies. Heavyweights like T1 and Gen.G are known to quickly adapt to these changes, using their deep strategic understanding to turn new circumstances to their advantage. Legendary midlaner Faker and other superstars will undoubtedly play a pivotal role in deciding how these changes alter the balance of power in South Korea's premier league.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'DK Career: "No matter who's evaluating us, we always think we're the best team"  Sheep Esports' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: Tarzan Shares His Top 3 Junglers In The World - EWC 2026 Interview
summary: Tarzan Shares His Top 3 Junglers In The World - EWC 2026 Interview  Hotspawn
content:
Even though it was a short run for AG.AL and Tarzan, we were able to catch up with the Korean jungler to hear his top three junglers in the world and what he wants to play when League Classic goes live.

Tarzan: HLE Kanavi, BLG Xun, andT1 Oner.

Tarzan: To be honest, I had not thought about it that seriously, so I cannot choose one. Maybe Faker once again?

Tarzan: As LoL is the game which do patches on every season, so many things are being changed periodically. So I think it is most important to be open-minded on LOL and practice in various ways.

Tarzan: I haven’t had a chance to playLeague Classicyet, but if I can, then I want to try. I want to play Classic Graves.

Tarzan: To be honest, there is not enough time to prepare forEWC, and EWC itself is a short-period match. So I think EWC’s fun and important part is that you need to make reviews on-time live, and you can prepare strategies in a short time, and my team can grow fast during EWC.

title: Gen.G defeat T1 to secure third place at the League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026
summary: Gen.G defeat T1 to secure third place at the League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026  GosuGamers
content:
Gen.G takes third place at LoL EWC 2026 (Image: Esports World Cup).

One of the fiercest rivalries inLeague of Legends(LoL) took centre stage once again in Paris. With the championship no longer within reach,T1andGen.Ginstead battled for the final place on the podium at the LoLEsports World Cup (EWC) 2026.

Having fallen toKarmine CorpandDplus KIA, respectively, in thesemifinals, the two Korean giants traded victories through the opening two games to face off for one final time. In the end, it was Gen.G that prevailed against T1 2-1 to claim third place.

Gen.G made the stronger start to the series, capitalising on T1's mistakes to steadily build control of the map.Jeong “Chovy” Ji-hoonled the way as Gen.G repeatedly found favourable skirmishes before breaking into T1's base with a commanding advantage.

T1 managed to buy themselves a little more time with a desperate Nexus defence, but Gen.G never loosened their grip on the game. Maintaining control of every major objective, Gen.G eventually overwhelmed the defending world champions to move within one win of third place. After a 27-minute encounter, Gen.G destroyed T1's Nexus with a 10.6k gold lead and a 25-9 kill lead to take a 1-0 series lead.

Facing elimination, T1 responded with a composed performance in the second game. While Gen.G controlled the early neutral objectives by securing three Void Grubs and the opening drakes, T1 gradually wrestled momentum away through cleaner team fighting and sharper map control. Once the defending world champions found their footing, the game quickly swung in their favour. Consecutive Baron buffs allowed T1 to dismantle Gen.G's remaining structures before marching into the LCK champions' base to level the series.

title: Karmine Corp, Dplus set to face off in Esports World Cup LOL final
summary: Karmine Corp, Dplus set to face off in Esports World Cup LOL final  Field Level Media
content:
Karmine Corp and Dplus will face off in the finals of the League of Legends event at the Esports World Cup in Paris on Sunday after winning their respective semifinals Saturday.

Karmine Corp pulled out a 2-1 defeat of T1, while Dplus outlasted Gen.G Esports for a 2-1 win. The two losing teams will open Sunday’s action with a third-place match.

The playoffs began Friday and conclude with Sunday’s best-of-three third-place match and best-of-five grand final. The winning team takes home $600,000 and 1,000 Esports World Cup club points. The MVP will earn an additional $25,000.

Karmine fell in a 1-0 hole in its match after T1 won the opener in 32 minutes on red. They rallied behind 30- and 38-minute victories, both on blue, to advance. Caliste “Caliste” Henry-Hennebert of France led Karmine Corp with the most kills (20) and fewest deaths (nine) in the match.

Dplus didn’t need to rally for their win, taking the opening game in 49 minutes on red and then coming out on top in the winner-take-all third game in 37 minutes on red after Gen.G leveled the score at 1-1 with a 41-minute triumph on red. Sin “Smash” Guem-jae led the all-South Korean Dplus team with a dominant showing, accounting for 26 of the team’s 56 kills and just five of their 42 deaths.

The Esports World Cup features competition in 25 titles and a $75 million prize pool. Other events this month include DOTA, PUBG, EA Sports FC, Call of Duty: Warzone, Call of Duty: Black Ops 7, Rocket League, Counter-Strike 2 and Fortnite.
